prompt: Please draft a research report analyzing future product development trends within the smart home industry. The report should conclude by identifying specific types of products, or products with particular features, that are expected to be major trends shaping the industry's future.
article:
## What “future product development trends” means here (assumptions)
- **Time horizon:** 2026–2030 (near-to-mid term), with references through 2032/2033 where that helps interpret direction.
- **Scope:** consumer smart home + “prosumer/SMB” adjacent deployments (multifamily, monitored security, insurer-loss-prevention), because these channels increasingly drive requirements and volumes.
- **What counts as a “trend”:** repeated signals across **standards roadmaps**, **platform requirements**, **device launches**, **retail/merchandising**, **regulation**, and **business-model shifts**.

---

## 1) 2024–2026 smart home baseline: growth, category mix, and where development is concentrating

### 1.1 Market trajectory: growth continues, but sources disagree because definitions differ
You should treat “smart home market size” as *model-dependent* (what’s included: TVs? services? security monitoring? energy services? etc.). Still, the **directional** picture is consistent: growth continues and shifts toward security, energy, and services.

**Shipments / device volume**
- IDC forecasts **0.6% growth** in global smart home device shipments in **2024** to **892.3M** units [[1]](https://my.idc.com/getdoc.jsp), with an IDC-attributed projection that emerging markets drive a rebound in **2025** to **931.1M** units (+4.4%) and a **2024–2028 CAGR of 5.6%** reaching **1.1B** by 2028 [[2]](https://backendnews.net/idc-emerging-regions-to-boost-smart-home-market-growth-by-2025/).  
- IDC commentary also highlights **security camera follow-on purchases** having higher upside than “another smart TV or smart speaker” as consumers progress in their smart home journey [[1]](https://my.idc.com/getdoc.jsp).

**Revenue / value**
- Statista Market Insights projects global smart home revenue of **US$193.5B in 2026** and **US$250.6B by 2029** (CAGR **8.99%**, 2026–2029) [[4]](https://www.statista.com/outlook/cmo/smart-home/worldwide), and explicitly includes device sales plus certain services (e.g., monitoring subscriptions) within its definition [[4]](https://www.statista.com/outlook/cmo/smart-home/worldwide).  
- Other analyst houses publish much larger or smaller totals (e.g., Grand View Research projecting **US$537.27B by 2030** [[8]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/press-release/global-smart-homes-market), Frost projecting **US$405.94B by 2030** [[12]](https://store.frost.com/research/smart-home-market), MarketsandMarkets projecting **US$139.24B by 2032** [[13]](https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/PressReleases/global-smart-homes-market.asp), Fortune Business Insights projecting **US$633.20B by 2032** [[15]](https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/press-release/smart-home-market-9476)). These numbers are **not directly comparable** due to scope and taxonomy differences [[8]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/press-release/global-smart-homes-market)[[12]](https://store.frost.com/research/smart-home-market)[[13]](https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/PressReleases/global-smart-homes-market.asp)[[15]](https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/press-release/smart-home-market-9476).

**Practical takeaway for product teams**
- Even if topline totals vary, the **investment heat** is clearly moving toward:  
  1) **security and risk reduction**,  
  2) **energy/electrification**,  
  3) **interoperability + simplified setup**, and  
  4) **service monetization** (subscriptions, monitoring, SaaS).

---

### 1.2 Category mix: security rises; speakers mature; appliances and energy become “platform” categories
**Security dominates incremental demand**
- IDC-attributed reporting: home security products (cameras and door locks) are **over 25% of shipments** [[2]](https://backendnews.net/idc-emerging-regions-to-boost-smart-home-market-growth-by-2025/).  
- Grand View Research: **security & access control** held **>29%** share in 2024 [[8]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/press-release/global-smart-homes-market), and was the largest revenue segment in both North America and Asia-Pacific in 2024 [[9]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/horizon/outlook/smart-home-market/north-america)[[10]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/horizon/outlook/smart-home-market/asia-pacific).  
- Within smart home security, cameras/monitoring were largest in 2024 and **smart locks** are forecast to grow fastest (2025–2030) [[11]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/smart-home-security-market-report).

**Smart speakers: maturity and decline in unit growth**
- IDC-attributed reporting: smart speakers are expected to **decline 8.8% in 2024**, with Google/Amazon prioritizing basic voice input and fewer extra features [[2]](https://backendnews.net/idc-emerging-regions-to-boost-smart-home-market-growth-by-2025/). This doesn’t mean “voice disappears”—rather it migrates into other devices (phones, TVs, hubs, appliances) and becomes more conversational via LLM assistants (see Section 3).

**Appliances and robotics: moving from vendor apps into ecosystems**
- Product/launch evidence shows major appliance makers and robot vacuum brands racing toward **Matter enablement** and richer autonomy (e.g., Bosch, Dreame, Eufy, SwitchBot) [[19]](https://matter-smarthome.de/en/products/an-overview-of-matter-innovations-at-ifa-2025/).  
- IDC-attributed reporting highlights AI transforming **smart vacuums** with object recognition and mopping improvements [[2]](https://backendnews.net/idc-emerging-regions-to-boost-smart-home-market-growth-by-2025/).

**Energy management becomes a primary smart home reason-to-buy**
- Matter 1.3/1.4 explicitly expand energy measurement and energy device types (EVSE, solar, batteries, heat pumps, water heaters) [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)[[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/), and CSA states home energy management is emerging as a top driver for adoption (citing Parks Associates) [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/).  
- Frost & Sullivan’s growth-opportunity framing highlights “Intelligent HEMS and integration with EVs” as new priorities [[12]](https://store.frost.com/research/smart-home-market).

---

### 1.3 Regional differences shaping product development
- **Asia-Pacific** is repeatedly positioned as the growth engine in some models: Grand View Research reports APAC **US$50.37B in 2024** rising to **US$231.04B by 2030** (CAGR **28.8%**, 2025–2030) [[10]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/horizon/outlook/smart-home-market/asia-pacific) and accounting for **39.4%** of global smart home revenue in 2024 [[10]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/horizon/outlook/smart-home-market/asia-pacific).  
- IDC expects **emerging markets** to drive a rebound in shipments starting 2025 [[2]](https://backendnews.net/idc-emerging-regions-to-boost-smart-home-market-growth-by-2025/).  
**Implication:** product development will prioritize **cost-down SKUs**, **Wi‑Fi-first Matter devices**, and **retail-friendly DIY setup**, while still supporting premium ecosystems in North America/Europe (professional monitoring, insurer programs, multifamily dashboards).

---

## 2) Interoperability and platform shifts reshaping product requirements (Matter/Thread/Wi‑Fi/Zigbee/Z‑Wave + ecosystems)

### 2.1 Matter’s evolution: from “basic devices” to energy, appliances, infrastructure, and sensing
Matter is an **IP-based application layer** standard to enable interoperable smart home devices over IP networks [[28]](https://csa-iot.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/matter-1-3-core-specification.pdf)[[29]](https://csa-iot.org/all-solutions/matter/). Matter devices communicate via **IPv6** over Wi‑Fi/Ethernet or Thread [[30]](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/matter/), and Google explicitly warns that lack of IPv6 can cause Matter control to fail over time [[31]](https://support.google.com/googlenest/answer/12391458).

**Matter 1.3 (2024): energy + water + major appliances + UX improvements**
CSA states Matter 1.3 introduced:
- **Energy reporting** (power/voltage/current; energy over time) [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- **EVSE support** (start/stop, rate control, “miles by departure time,” optimized charging windows) [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- **Water management** (leak/freeze detectors, rain sensors, water valves) [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- **Major appliances** (microwaves/ovens/cooktops/hoods/dryers) [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- UX/automation improvements: **Scenes** and **Command Batching** to reduce “popcorn effect” in lighting and improve responsiveness [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- Better commissioning (directed scan, Thread attributes, improved setup error reporting) [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)

**Matter 1.4 (late 2024): network infrastructure + multi-admin + energy device types + improved sensing + battery devices**
CSA states Matter 1.4 adds:
- **Enhanced Multi-Admin**: single-consent device sharing across ecosystems [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- **Home Routers and Access Points (HRAP)**: certifiable Wi‑Fi AP + Thread Border Router devices with secure Thread credential storage/sharing to reduce Thread fragmentation [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- Energy device types: **solar**, **batteries (BESS)**, **heat pumps**, **water heaters**, plus improvements for **thermostats** and **device energy management** modes [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- **Occupancy sensing** enhancements supporting radar/vision/ambient sensing and better tuning/history [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- Battery-powered device improvements for **Intermittently Connected Devices (ICDs)** via Long Idle Time protocol and Check-In protocol [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)

**Implication for product roadmaps (2026–2030)**
- Categories that become **first-class Matter device types** tend to attract faster cross-brand adoption because they become addressable by Apple/Google/Amazon/Samsung controllers with less custom integration effort.
- The fastest-growing “future categories” therefore skew toward **energy/electrification devices**, **infrastructure**, and **sensing**, not just lights/plugs.

---

### 2.2 Thread’s 1.4 transition: solving multi-network fragmentation (a major friction point)
Thread is the low-power IPv6 mesh typically used for Matter-over-Thread [[48]](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/thread/)[[50]](https://threadgroup.org/news-events/press-releases/ID/328/Thread-Group-Opens-Access-to-Third-Evolution-of-its-Wireless-Networking-Protocol-Enabling-Matter-Improving-Seamless-Connectivity-in-Smart-Homes-and-Buildings). Thread Group reports growing ecosystem scale (membership and >1,100 certified products) and continued spec work on commissioning, routing, and diagnostics [[21]](https://www.threadgroup.org/Portals/0/Documents/Newsroom/2H_2025_TG_Report_(Interest).pdf).

Thread 1.4 specifically introduces improvements including **credentials sharing** and better onboarding and infrastructure-network integration [[51]](https://www.threadgroup.org/Portals/0/Documents/Thread_1.4_Features_White_Paper_September_2024.pdf). Thread 1.4’s credential-sharing flow is designed to fix the “I can’t add a Thread device because I don’t have the Thread credentials” problem, which otherwise leads to fragmented meshes and poor reliability [[51]](https://www.threadgroup.org/Portals/0/Documents/Thread_1.4_Features_White_Paper_September_2024.pdf). Matter 1.4’s HRAP concept aligns with this direction by standardizing secure Thread credential storage/sharing [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/).

**Product implication:** 2026–2030 “best-in-class” smart home products will increasingly be judged on:
- **commissioning success rate**,  
- **mesh stability in dense homes**,  
- **diagnostic visibility**, and  
- **border-router interoperability**—not just device features.

---

### 2.3 Platform ecosystems: interoperability is rising, but each platform adds requirements and constraints

#### Apple Home
- Apple’s Matter accessory best practices (2025) instruct developers to use the **latest Matter SDK main branch certified with Matter 1.4**, implement **Matter OTA**, and implement diagnostic clusters/logging for field debugging [[36]](https://developer.apple.com/apple-home/downloads/Matter-Accessory-Best-Practices-for-Apple-Home.pdf).  
- “Works with Apple Home” badge requires CSA Matter certification + CSA interoperability testing, and Apple lists supported Matter categories (including locks, robot vacuums, sensors, switches, thermostats) [[37]](https://developer.apple.com/apple-home/works-with-apple-home/).  
**Implication:** “Apple-ready” products need strong OTA/diagnostics and Thread interoperability discipline.

#### Google Home
- Google stresses Matter’s local control benefits and requires appropriate hubs/border routers; it also emphasizes **IPv6** as necessary for robust Matter interoperability [[31]](https://support.google.com/googlenest/answer/12391458).  
- Google developer docs state that **starting in 2026**, Matter integration/testing will require the **Alliance Interop Test** [[41]](https://developers.home.google.com/matter/supported-devices).  
**Implication:** certification/testing overhead becomes more standardized and potentially more stringent, pushing vendors to invest in compliance engineering and test automation.

#### Amazon Alexa
- Amazon positions Matter as enabling direct local connectivity without a skill, reducing latency and improving reliability [[42]](https://developer.amazon.com/en-US/docs/alexa/smarthome/matter-support.html).  
- Amazon supports Thread credential sharing via its **Credential Locker API** (with customer permission) to streamline Matter-over-Thread setup by skills/hubs [[43]](https://developer.amazon.com/en-US/docs/alexa/smarthome/thread-support.html).  
**Implication:** device makers and service providers can reduce setup friction, but must design for permissioned credential handling and platform policies.

#### Samsung SmartThings
- Samsung explicitly commits to maintaining **Zigbee and Z‑Wave** support while transitioning to **Matter and Thread**, positioning itself as a multi-protocol bridge during the transition [[46]](https://news.samsung.com/global/samsung-electronics-launches-matter-functionality-ushering-a-new-era-of-interoperability).  
**Implication:** bridges/hubs remain important, especially for legacy devices and advanced features not yet in Matter.

#### Home Assistant (prosumer/local-first)
- Home Assistant emphasizes local control and multi-controller “multi-fabric” behavior, but notes that Matter may expose fewer features than vendor-native integrations (Hue example) [[30]](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/matter/).  
- It also documents real-world limitations (e.g., Matter OTA updates not working for Thread devices on Apple border routers in their current setup) [[30]](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/matter/).  
**Implication:** advanced users will continue to demand “local-first” and richer device capabilities beyond baseline Matter—pressuring vendors to keep local APIs or robust Matter feature coverage.

---

### 2.4 Zigbee and Z‑Wave: not “dead,” but repositioned

**Zigbee**
- CSA reports **over one billion Zigbee chipsets sold**, and thousands of certified Zigbee products [[54]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/the-connectivity-standards-alliance-introduces-zigbee-direct-simplifying-integration-with-bluetooth-low-energy-devices).  
- Zigbee Direct adds BLE-based onboarding/control to Zigbee devices without cloud/hub, blending BLE ubiquity with Zigbee mesh [[54]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/the-connectivity-standards-alliance-introduces-zigbee-direct-simplifying-integration-with-bluetooth-low-energy-devices)[[55]](https://csa-iot.org/all-solutions/zigbee/zigbee-direct-faq/).  
**Implication:** Zigbee ecosystems will persist, especially where cost, installed base, and mesh performance matter, and Zigbee Direct can reduce onboarding friction.

**Z‑Wave**
- Z‑Wave Alliance cites **>4,500 certified devices** and emphasizes Z‑Wave Long Range for MDUs/hospitality with up to ~1.5 mile range [[57]](https://z-wavealliance.org/news_p/z-wave-protocol-certification-achieved-by-silicon-labs-and-trident-iot/).  
**Implication:** Z‑Wave remains relevant in professional/residential security and property deployments where range/backward compatibility matter.

---

### 2.5 Wi‑Fi evolution: Wi‑Fi 6/6E/7 improves reliability, density, and latency; Wi‑Fi power efficiency improves battery feasibility
- Wi‑Fi Alliance’s Wi‑Fi CERTIFIED 7 highlights deterministic latency, higher throughput, and reliability features like Multi‑Link Operation [[52]](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/01/08/2805409/0/en/Wi-Fi-Alliance-introduces-Wi-Fi-CERTIFIED-7.html)[[53]](https://next.ink/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Wi-Fi_CERTIFIED_7_Technology_Overview.pdf).  
- Silicon Labs and Espressif explicitly emphasize Wi‑Fi 6 **Target Wake Time (TWT)** as enabling multi-year battery life for always-connected devices [[70]](https://news.silabs.com/2024-12-03-Silicon-Labs-Breakthrough-Ultra-Low-Power-Wi-Fi-6-and-Bluetooth-LE-5-4-Modules-Supercharge-Device-Deployment)[[73]](https://www.espressif.com/en/products/socs/esp32-c6).  
**Implication:** many “traditionally Thread/Zigbee-only” device types (locks, sensors, doorbells) can increasingly be built as **battery-efficient Wi‑Fi Matter devices**, expanding addressable markets and simplifying BOM in some designs.

---

## 3) Technology enablers shaping next-gen smart home products (and what they unlock)

### 3.1 Edge AI + multimodal assistants (LLMs) shift the UI from “commands” to “intent”
Qualcomm frames 2025+ smart home as a transition to on-device AI capable of complex tasks, enabling faster, more secure, personalized experiences when processed locally [[59]](https://www.qualcomm.com/news/onq/2025/01/edge-intelligence-and-interoperability-key-components-driving-next-chapter-of-smart-home). Qualcomm demonstrations include:
- appliance chatbots with recipe recommendations based on **computer vision ingredient detection** [[59]](https://www.qualcomm.com/news/onq/2025/01/edge-intelligence-and-interoperability-key-components-driving-next-chapter-of-smart-home)  
- TV-centric multimodal control using camera + microphone for CV + speech, controlling smart home devices and enabling translation, personalization, etc. [[59]](https://www.qualcomm.com/news/onq/2025/01/edge-intelligence-and-interoperability-key-components-driving-next-chapter-of-smart-home)  
- home service robots using natural language + vision to identify household objects [[59]](https://www.qualcomm.com/news/onq/2025/01/edge-intelligence-and-interoperability-key-components-driving-next-chapter-of-smart-home)

Google’s Gemini for Home similarly pushes:
- natural-language automation creation (“type a question… or an automation idea… let Gemini do it”) [[63]](https://home.google.com/get-inspired/the-new-google-home-built-around-you/)  
- richer camera semantics (e.g., more specific descriptions than “person detected”) and daily summaries (“Home Brief”) [[63]](https://home.google.com/get-inspired/the-new-google-home-built-around-you/)

**Implication for product development:** the winning products will expose **structured events and controllable capabilities** that AI systems can reliably compose into routines—while minimizing false positives and respecting privacy (see Section 4).

---

### 3.2 Computer vision moves from “recording” to “understanding + retrieval”
Google’s refreshed Nest cameras emphasize sensor choices that support AI interpretation “now and going forward,” with semantic scene understanding enabling richer notifications and user queries (e.g., “what time did the kids get home?”) [[64]](https://techcrunch.com/2025/10/01/google-unveils-ai-powered-nest-indoor-and-outdoor-cameras-and-a-new-doorbell/). This shifts product requirements toward:
- better sensors/optics for AI (resolution, FoV, low light) [[64]](https://techcrunch.com/2025/10/01/google-unveils-ai-powered-nest-indoor-and-outdoor-cameras-and-a-new-doorbell/)
- on-device or edge-assisted inference pipelines
- searchable event embeddings (often subscription-tied)

Amazon/Ring similarly trends toward AI video features as subscription value (e.g., Smart Video Search) [[20]](https://techcrunch.com/2024/10/09/amazon-revamps-ring-subscriptions-with-ai-video-search/).

---

### 3.3 UWB enables “ambient” secure access and presence-aware experiences
Aqara’s U400 smart lock uses **UWB Time-of-Flight + Angle-of-Arrival** to do hands-free unlocking only when approaching from outside, reducing accidental unlocks common in Bluetooth proximity methods [[67]](https://www.aqara.com/us/news-us-2/aqara-introduces-uwb-powered-smart-lock-u400-at-ces-2026/). It also positions UWB as more resistant to relay attacks via precise ranging and STS security features [[67]](https://www.aqara.com/us/news-us-2/aqara-introduces-uwb-powered-smart-lock-u400-at-ces-2026/).  
FiRa’s certification work (Core 3.0, CCC Digital Key UWB) shows ecosystem maturation toward interoperable UWB deployments [[69]](https://www.firaconsortium.org/sites/default/files/2025-02/2024%20FiRa%20Annual%20Report%20-%20Final_0.pdf).

**Implication:** expect a wave of **UWB-enabled locks** and, more broadly, UWB presence/location features for home interactions where “where are you relative to the door/room?” matters.

---

### 3.4 Low-power silicon expands what can be battery-powered (and always connected)
- Silicon Labs’ Wi‑Fi 6 + BLE 5.4 modules (SiWx917Y) target low-power smart home use cases, claiming connected sleep currents as low as 20 µA using TWT with a 60s keep-alive interval and enabling multi-year battery life for devices including locks/thermostats/cameras/doorbells (vendor claim) [[70]](https://news.silabs.com/2024-12-03-Silicon-Labs-Breakthrough-Ultra-Low-Power-Wi-Fi-6-and-Bluetooth-LE-5-4-Modules-Supercharge-Device-Deployment).  
- Espressif’s ESP32‑C6 combines Wi‑Fi 6 + BLE + 802.15.4 (Thread/Zigbee) and highlights TWT as enabling years-long battery operation while staying connected [[73]](https://www.espressif.com/en/products/socs/esp32-c6).  
**Implication:** product categories that historically required Thread/Zigbee for battery life now have **credible Wi‑Fi options**, changing architecture and cost tradeoffs.

---

### 3.5 Energy harvesting and “batteryless” sensors reduce maintenance and unlock dense sensing
EnOcean’s self-powered sensor family illustrates how a single batteryless device can provide occupancy/light/activity signals for automation and space analytics [[75]](https://www.enocean.com/en/smart-building/motion-and-activity-sensor/).  
**Implication:** the cost of maintaining sensors (battery replacement, service calls) is a major barrier in both homes and multifamily/buildings; batteryless or ultra-long-life sensors unlock higher sensor density and better automation fidelity.

---

### 3.6 Digital twins: a path to predictive home energy and maintenance (but interoperability and privacy remain obstacles)
A 2025 review describes building digital twins as combining BIM static data with real-time IoT sensor data and AI to enable monitoring, predictive modeling, renewable integration, and proactive maintenance—while flagging challenges including interoperability, scalability, and privacy [[77]](https://www.mdpi.com/2075-5309/15/3/498).  
**Implication:** “digital twin” concepts will increasingly appear in higher-end homes and multifamily operator platforms first (where ROI is clearer), then trickle into consumer HEMS products.

---

### 3.7 Robotics: from cleaning to broader home service (longer term)
Robot vacuums are already integrating AI navigation and moving toward Matter ecosystem control [[19]](https://matter-smarthome.de/en/products/an-overview-of-matter-innovations-at-ifa-2025/). Qualcomm’s home service robot demos show a longer-term trajectory toward multi-purpose robots combining LLM interaction + vision + edge compute [[59]](https://www.qualcomm.com/news/onq/2025/01/edge-intelligence-and-interoperability-key-components-driving-next-chapter-of-smart-home).  
**Implication:** near-term (2026–2030) robotics growth concentrates on **floor care and lawn care**, plus incremental “manipulation” features; broader home service robots are plausible but less certain and more premium.

---

## 4) Security, privacy, safety, and regulation: how they reshape smart home product design (2024–2030)

### 4.1 A wave of IoT cybersecurity regulation/labeling is making “security-by-default + update commitments” non-negotiable

**United States (FCC Cyber Trust Mark)**
- FCC adopted a voluntary IoT cybersecurity labeling program (Cyber Trust Mark + QR code) to provide baseline cybersecurity assurances and encourage security-by-design [[87]](https://docs.fcc.gov/public/attachments/FCC-24-26A1.pdf).  
- Program registry disclosures include whether updates are automatic, minimum support period end date, and whether the manufacturer maintains HBOM/SBOM [[92]](https://cdn.cta.tech/cta/media/media/pdfs/cta-cyber-trust-mark-webinar-part-i_june-2024_fcc_and_cta.pdf).  
**Design impact:** vendors will need documented support windows, patch pipelines, and SBOM practices to be competitive in retail and procurement.

**EU (Cyber Resilience Act + RED cybersecurity deadlines)**
- CRA mandates essential cybersecurity requirements such as secure-by-default configuration, vulnerability handling, encryption, and data minimization [[91]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng).  
- Key timeline: vulnerability and incident reporting obligations begin **Sept 2026**, with full application **Dec 2027** [[88]](https://www.hoganlovells.com/en/publications/eu-cyber-resilience-act-getting-ready-for-cra-compliance-in-2026).  
- Separately, RED cybersecurity requirements for certain radio equipment apply from **Aug 1, 2025**, creating earlier compliance pressure for many smart-home radio devices [[100]](https://www.reedsmith.com/our-insights/blogs/viewpoints/102ktki/red-eu-cybersecurity-obligations-for-iot-products-from-august-2025/). Harmonised standards EN 18031-x were added in 2025, with explicit concerns about password clauses that allow “no password,” limiting presumption of conformity in those cases [[101]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dec_impl/2025/138/oj/eng).  
**Design impact:** password/authentication rules, secure update design, and privacy protections become tied to market access.

**UK (PSTI)**
- Effective **April 29, 2024**, mandates: no universal default passwords, a vulnerability reporting point of contact, and published minimum security update periods [[89]](https://www.gov.uk/guidance/regulations-consumer-connectable-product-security). The Regulations detail password uniqueness and support-period publication rules [[90]](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2023/1007/schedule/1).  
**Design impact:** the UK makes lifecycle security transparency a legal requirement; short support windows become a compliance and reputational liability.

**Singapore (CLS)**
- CLS covers hubs, routers, IP cameras, smart locks, smart lights, etc., with Levels 1–4 escalating from unique passwords + updates to secure-by-design, binary assessment, and pen testing [[93]](https://www.csa.gov.sg/our-programmes/certification-and-labelling-schemes/cybersecurity-labelling-scheme/about/)[[94]](https://www.csa.gov.sg/our-programmes/certification-and-labelling-schemes/cybersecurity-labelling-scheme/for-consumers/).  
**Design impact:** security labeling becomes a competitive differentiator; MRAs reduce duplicative testing but raise baseline expectations [[93]](https://www.csa.gov.sg/our-programmes/certification-and-labelling-schemes/cybersecurity-labelling-scheme/about/).

**Baseline standards convergence**
- ETSI EN 303 645 provides a widely referenced baseline including no universal default passwords, vulnerability disclosure, secure updates, secure communications, minimizing attack surface, and user data deletion [[95]](https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_en/303600_303699/303645/03.01.03_60/en_303645v030103p.pdf)[[104]](https://www.etsi.org/technologies/consumer-iot-security).  
**Design impact:** these controls increasingly shape “table stakes” across regions.

---

### 4.2 Privacy pressures: cameras/mics and “smart sensing” face increasing scrutiny
- EDPB guidelines on video surveillance emphasize data minimization, specific purpose limitation, and warn that “smart cameras” and advanced video analytics increase risks and misuse potential [[96]](https://www.edpb.europa.eu/sites/default/files/files/file1/edpb_guidelines_201903_video_devices_en_0.pdf). It also notes household exemption limitations when cameras capture public spaces [[96]](https://www.edpb.europa.eu/sites/default/files/files/file1/edpb_guidelines_201903_video_devices_en_0.pdf).  
- GDPR “data protection by design and by default” (Article 25) requires technical/organizational measures to enforce data minimization by default [[105]](https://gdpr-info.eu/art-25-gdpr/).  
**Design impact:** expect product differentiation around **local processing**, **shorter retention defaults**, **granular privacy zones**, and **transparent data practices**—and more demand for privacy-preserving “on-device AI.”

---

### 4.3 Security-by-design meets smart home standards: attestation, provisioning, and OTA become core competencies
Matter certification has operational implications:
- Matter devices require provisioning of attestation materials (DAC/PAI/PAA chain), certification declarations, and onboarding credentials; certification declarations are issued per certified firmware update, so update strategy affects certification planning [[35]](https://handbook.buildwithmatter.com/certification/certifying-a-product/development/).  
- Apple specifically recommends implementing Matter OTA and diagnostics/logging for field investigations [[36]](https://developer.apple.com/apple-home/downloads/Matter-Accessory-Best-Practices-for-Apple-Home.pdf).  
**Design impact:** product development shifts from “ship firmware once” to continuous compliance, with secure provisioning and recertification baked into release engineering.

---

### 4.4 Insurance and monitoring certifications influence security and loss-prevention product requirements
- UL Solutions describes security alarm service certification and notes insurers may require features such as line security for certain systems [[97]](https://www.ul.com/resources/security-alarm-service-certification).  
**Design impact:** professionally monitored security ecosystems will continue to require compliance, auditability, and robust service operations, influencing hardware design (panels, sensors), installation flows, and backend reliability.

---

## 5) Jobs-to-be-done driving adoption (and the business models that follow)

### 5.1 Home security outcomes (safety, deterrence, verification) + subscription monetization
Security remains a primary “why buy” driver and a major subscription engine:
- Ring documents plan migrations and ties features like professional monitoring and AI features to specific subscription tiers [[134]](https://ring.com/support/articles/7zvp5/Ring-Protect-Plus-1st-Gen-Information)[[135]](https://ring.com/explore-ring-home).  
- Google transitions Nest Aware (2nd gen) to **Google Home Premium** (Oct 1, 2025) and positions Gemini-enabled features (richer alerts, search) as subscription value, with tiers and pricing [[136]](https://support.google.com/googlenest/answer/16581343)[[137]](https://home.google.com/get-inspired/welcome-to-google-home-premium-the-new-era-of-nest-aware/).  
- Arlo reports strong subscription/services economics (ARR and subscription revenue growth) and partnerships like Comcast/Xfinity distribution [[163]](https://www.marketscreener.com/news/arlo-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results-ce7e5cd9d189f42d).  
**Business model trend:** hardware becomes the “endpoint,” while AI search, longer video history, verification, and emergency response become recurring services.

---

### 5.2 Energy cost volatility + electrification (EVs, heat pumps) + demand response
Utilities are expanding demand response and managed charging:
- Utility industry commentary describes expanding DR beyond summer peaks and using smart thermostats as the gateway for broader load control, with EVs becoming a flexible load for managed charging [[129]](https://www.clearesult.com/insights/expanding-demand-response-and-managed-charging-for-year-round-grid-stability).  
- Utility programs show real mechanisms: pre-cooling and setpoint adjustments during events, opt-outs, and incentives; some programs incorporate thermostats, batteries, EV chargers, and even Wi‑Fi air conditioners [[130]](https://neep.org/sites/default/files/media-files/residential_programs_tracker.pdf).  
- Example managed charging program: Clark Public Utilities + Optiwatt provides bill credits, scheduled charging events, and telematics integration through 2026 [[131]](https://www.clarkpublicutilities.com/residential-customers/reduce-energy-waste-and-lower-your-bill/all-rebates-incentives-and-low-interest-loans/electric-vehicle-managed-charging-program/).  
**Business model trend:** smart home energy products increasingly monetize via **utility incentives**, **tariffs**, and potentially **grid services (VPPs)**, in addition to device sales.

---

### 5.3 Aging-in-place + caregiver shortage + home-based care
Reports highlight older adults’ needs across safety/security, health/wellness, and communication:
- Smart home tech can support independence and caregiver burden reduction, but barriers include usability fears, reliability concerns, and privacy/security concerns [[124]](https://agetechcollaborative.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/ATC_-Smart-Home-Report_R1.2.pdf).  
- 2025 market overview expects smart displays and voice interactions to be ubiquitous and highlights shrinking sensor form factors and ambient listening/fall detection trends [[125]](https://www.ageinplacetech.com/files/aip/Market%20Overview%202025%20%20January%202025.pdf).  
**Product trend:** growth in **passive monitoring**, **non-wearable fall detection**, **simple voice/display hubs**, and caregiver-friendly remote configuration—tempered by privacy constraints and trust requirements.

---

### 5.4 Multifamily / property management: centralized operations, risk reduction (water), energy optimization, and SaaS
- Multifamily operators face rising costs and use smart thermostats and leak detection for vacancy management, risk mitigation, and predictive maintenance [[143]](https://www.resideo.com/us/en/corporate/newsroom/featured-articles/Why-smart-home-technology-is-a-win-win-for-multifamily-operators-and-residents/).  
- ADT positions smart locks/thermostats/leak sensors as tools to reduce rekeying, manage energy in vacant units, and reduce damage risk [[144]](https://newsroom.adt.com/smart-stories/why-smart-tech-is-the-secret-weapon-for-multifamily-property-managers-in-2025).  
- SmartRent’s financials show the model shifting toward SaaS: SaaS revenue ~37% of total in Q2 2025 (up from 26%), ARR up to $56.9M, and “Units Deployed” ~847,956 [[146]](https://investors.smartrent.com/news/news-details/2025/SmartRent-Reports-Second-Quarter-2025-Financial-Results/).  
**Business model trend:** “smart home” in multifamily is increasingly a **B2B SaaS + deployment** business, not just device retail.

---

### 5.5 Insurer-driven loss prevention: leak detection, fire/smoke, and monitored systems
- Nationwide + Resideo partnership aims to shift from reactive claims to proactive prevention; preferred device packages may come with insurance discounts [[140]](https://news.nationwide.com/nationwide-and-resideo-join-to-help-protect-homes/)[[141]](https://www.dig-in.com/news/nationwide-resideo-partner-on-smart-home-device-discounts).  
- Nationwide + Phyn partnership offers discounts for leak detection/shutoff solutions for high-value homes [[142]](https://news.nationwide.com/nationwide-and-phyn-partner-to-offer-advanced-water-damageprotection-for-homeowners/).  
**Product trend:** strong pull for **connected water shutoff**, **leak detection**, **monitored smoke/CO**, and **data-sharing frameworks** (with privacy controls) tied to premium discounts.

---

## 6) Future product development trends (2026–2030): what will shape the industry, and why

Below is a **ranked** set of product trends, with the specific product types and features most likely to dominate product roadmaps. Rankings reflect strength of evidence across standards, platforms, launches, retail signals, and business-model pull.

---

## Trend 1 (Highest impact): Home Energy Management Systems (HEMS) + “energy-aware” devices as core smart home platform
**Why this dominates**
- Matter 1.3/1.4 make energy measurement and energy device types first-class (EVSE, solar, batteries, heat pumps, water heaters, device energy management modes) [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)[[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/). CSA explicitly frames home energy management as a top driver for smart home adoption (Parks Associates) [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/).  
- Real-world demos and early products appear (EcoFlow’s HEMS concepts; “certified HEMS” claims) [[152]](https://matter-smarthome.de/en/products/the-matter-innovations-at-ces-2025/).  
- Retail signals show strong demand for home energy monitors (Emporia) [[153]](https://www.amazon.ca/Best-Sellers-Home-Automation/zgbs/hi/9716053011).

**Product types most likely to surge**
1) **HEMS controllers/apps** that orchestrate tariffs, comfort, and device scheduling (often with subscriptions).  
2) **Energy-reporting endpoints**: smart plugs, appliance controllers, in-wall load controllers, EV chargers, thermostats, water heaters, batteries.  
3) **HEMS-integrated dashboards** for consumers and property operators.

**Feature set to expect**
- Matter-based **power/energy telemetry**, per-device attribution and forecasting [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- **Automated scheduling** (cheapest/lowest-carbon windows; demand-response compliance) [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)[[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- Integration with **EVSE** and “departure time” charging [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- Privacy-preserving analytics: local processing where possible + clear consent for utility/insurer sharing (driven by regulatory pressure) [[91]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng)[[92]](https://cdn.cta.tech/cta/media/media/pdfs/cta-cyber-trust-mark-webinar-part-i_june-2024_fcc_and_cta.pdf)[[96]](https://www.edpb.europa.eu/sites/default/files/files/file1/edpb_guidelines_201903_video_devices_en_0.pdf)

---

## Trend 2: Smart electrical infrastructure—smart panels, circuit-level monitoring, and “managed power” (electrification readiness)
**Why this dominates**
- Electrification adds large loads (EVs, heat pumps). Matter 1.4 adds more accurate in-wall “Mounted Load Control” device types for wired loads [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/).  
- Patents and ongoing innovation exist around load disaggregation and smart breaker identification [[158]](https://patents.google.com/patent/US20240080365A1/en).  
- Retail/home improvement channels increasingly treat EV charging as part of smart energy management [[154]](https://www.homedepot.com/b/Smart-Home-Smart-Energy-Management/N-5yc1vZc7cj).

**Product types**
- **Smart electrical panels / load centers** and circuit monitors  
- **In-wall load controllers** for hardwired loads (fans, pumps, heaters), not just “lights” [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- **Whole-home energy monitor + control bundles**

**Features**
- Circuit-level **measurement + control**, automated load shedding, outage modes  
- Strong **cybersecurity posture** (because panels become critical infrastructure in the home) aligned with CRA/RED/PSTI-style requirements [[91]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng)[[100]](https://www.reedsmith.com/our-insights/blogs/viewpoints/102ktki/red-eu-cybersecurity-obligations-for-iot-products-from-august-2025/)[[90]](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2023/1007/schedule/1)

---

## Trend 3: Smart EV charging (EVSE) as a first-class smart home device category, integrated into HEMS and grid programs
**Why**
- Matter 1.3/1.4 standardize EVSE capabilities and preferences, explicitly describing optimized charging windows and user-defined charging goals [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)[[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/).  
- Utility managed charging programs are expanding and require compatibility clarity and opt-out mechanisms [[129]](https://www.clearesult.com/insights/expanding-demand-response-and-managed-charging-for-year-round-grid-stability)[[131]](https://www.clarkpublicutilities.com/residential-customers/reduce-energy-waste-and-lower-your-bill/all-rebates-incentives-and-low-interest-loans/electric-vehicle-managed-charging-program/).

**Product types**
- **Matter-capable EV chargers** (EVSE)  
- EVSE bundled with **smart panels**, batteries, or HEMS subscriptions  
- Utility-compatible managed charging solutions (telematics, Wi‑Fi, demand-response readiness)

**Features**
- Departure-time / range-based charging goals [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- Grid/price-aware scheduling; demand-response event handling [[129]](https://www.clearesult.com/insights/expanding-demand-response-and-managed-charging-for-year-round-grid-stability)[[130]](https://neep.org/sites/default/files/media-files/residential_programs_tracker.pdf)  
- Clear user control and transparency (opt-out, privacy consent), shaped by policy expectations [[87]](https://docs.fcc.gov/public/attachments/FCC-24-26A1.pdf)[[91]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng)

---

## Trend 4: Electrified HVAC + water heating becomes an ecosystem anchor (not just a thermostat)
**Why**
- Matter 1.4 adds heat pumps and water heaters and improves thermostat scheduling/presets and device energy management [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/).  
- Utilities already treat thermostats as the “gateway” DR device and a stepping stone to broader load control [[129]](https://www.clearesult.com/insights/expanding-demand-response-and-managed-charging-for-year-round-grid-stability).

**Product types**
- **Next-gen thermostats** that act as multi-protocol hubs/controllers (Thread/Zigbee/Wi‑Fi)  
- **Matter-enabled heat pump controllers** and **smart water heaters**  
- Retrofit-friendly devices designed for installer + utility programs

**Features**
- Preset modes, scheduling, and automation triggers (home/away/vacation) [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- Forecast-aware control (pre-heat/pre-cool, shifting load) [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- Energy telemetry + device energy management modes [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)

---

## Trend 5: Security shifts toward AI-driven “understanding,” verification, and service bundles (subscriptions keep growing)
**Why**
- Security is already the leading shipment/revenue category in multiple sources [[2]](https://backendnews.net/idc-emerging-regions-to-boost-smart-home-market-growth-by-2025/)[[8]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/press-release/global-smart-homes-market)[[9]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/horizon/outlook/smart-home-market/north-america)[[10]](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/horizon/outlook/smart-home-market/asia-pacific).  
- Subscription restructuring and monetization are explicit at Ring and Google [[134]](https://ring.com/support/articles/7zvp5/Ring-Protect-Plus-1st-Gen-Information)[[136]](https://support.google.com/googlenest/answer/16581343)[[137]](https://home.google.com/get-inspired/welcome-to-google-home-premium-the-new-era-of-nest-aware/).  
- AI features (search, semantic alerts, summaries) increasingly differentiate tiers [[20]](https://techcrunch.com/2024/10/09/amazon-revamps-ring-subscriptions-with-ai-video-search/)[[64]](https://techcrunch.com/2025/10/01/google-unveils-ai-powered-nest-indoor-and-outdoor-cameras-and-a-new-doorbell/)[[136]](https://support.google.com/googlenest/answer/16581343).

**Product types**
- Cameras/doorbells with **AI search and semantic events**  
- **Hybrid security systems** blending DIY hardware with professional monitoring  
- Multifamily security + access bundles

**Features**
- Natural-language event search and richer alert semantics [[20]](https://techcrunch.com/2024/10/09/amazon-revamps-ring-subscriptions-with-ai-video-search/)[[64]](https://techcrunch.com/2025/10/01/google-unveils-ai-powered-nest-indoor-and-outdoor-cameras-and-a-new-doorbell/)  
- “Daily brief” / summaries and context-based notifications [[63]](https://home.google.com/get-inspired/the-new-google-home-built-around-you/)[[64]](https://techcrunch.com/2025/10/01/google-unveils-ai-powered-nest-indoor-and-outdoor-cameras-and-a-new-doorbell/)  
- Strong compliance posture for IoT labels and regulations (unique creds, update commitments, vulnerability reporting) [[87]](https://docs.fcc.gov/public/attachments/FCC-24-26A1.pdf)[[90]](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2023/1007/schedule/1)[[91]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng)[[92]](https://cdn.cta.tech/cta/media/media/pdfs/cta-cyber-trust-mark-webinar-part-i_june-2024_fcc_and_cta.pdf)

---

## Trend 6: Network + hub infrastructure becomes a product category again (HRAP routers, Thread border routers, controllers), because it reduces friction
**Why**
- Matter 1.4 introduces HRAP to standardize Wi‑Fi AP + Thread border router and reduce Thread fragmentation via credential sharing [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/).  
- Thread ecosystem is expanding and focuses on commissioning/diagnostics improvements [[21]](https://www.threadgroup.org/Portals/0/Documents/Newsroom/2H_2025_TG_Report_(Interest).pdf)[[51]](https://www.threadgroup.org/Portals/0/Documents/Thread_1.4_Features_White_Paper_September_2024.pdf).  
- Retail best-seller lists show hubs/controllers remain a strong category (Hue bridges, Aqara hubs) [[153]](https://www.amazon.ca/Best-Sellers-Home-Automation/zgbs/hi/9716053011)[[168]](https://www.amazon.ca/Best-Sellers-Home-Automation-Hubs-Controllers/zgbs/hi/9716054011).

**Product types**
- **Matter-certified home routers/access points (HRAP)**  
- **Smart displays and hubs** that act as Matter controllers + border routers  
- Multi-protocol bridges supporting Zigbee/Z‑Wave legacy plus Matter

**Features**
- Credential sharing, unified Thread mesh, improved commissioning success [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)[[51]](https://www.threadgroup.org/Portals/0/Documents/Thread_1.4_Features_White_Paper_September_2024.pdf)  
- Local automation execution + diagnostics/logging (platform best practices) [[36]](https://developer.apple.com/apple-home/downloads/Matter-Accessory-Best-Practices-for-Apple-Home.pdf)

---

## Trend 7: Digital access convergence—UWB/NFC locks + cross-ecosystem credential standards (Aliro)
**Why**
- UWB locks show tangible UX benefit: hands-free unlock with trajectory awareness [[67]](https://www.aqara.com/us/news-us-2/aqara-introduces-uwb-powered-smart-lock-u400-at-ces-2026/).  
- CSA’s Aliro initiative aims to standardize credentials/communications for access readers using NFC/BLE/UWB and asymmetric cryptography [[169]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/the-connectivity-standards-alliance-announces-aliro-a-new-effort-to-make-mobile-devices-wearables-central-to-a-digital-access-future/).

**Product types**
- Smart locks with **UWB + NFC + biometrics** and Matter/Thread connectivity [[67]](https://www.aqara.com/us/news-us-2/aqara-introduces-uwb-powered-smart-lock-u400-at-ces-2026/)  
- Access ecosystems designed for **wallet keys** and multi-platform compatibility

**Features**
- Precise ranging + approach direction to reduce false unlocks [[67]](https://www.aqara.com/us/news-us-2/aqara-introduces-uwb-powered-smart-lock-u400-at-ces-2026/)  
- Cross-platform access credential interoperability (Aliro direction) [[169]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/the-connectivity-standards-alliance-announces-aliro-a-new-effort-to-make-mobile-devices-wearables-central-to-a-digital-access-future/)  
- Strong security posture (anti-relay, secure provisioning, long update support) [[67]](https://www.aqara.com/us/news-us-2/aqara-introduces-uwb-powered-smart-lock-u400-at-ces-2026/)[[90]](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2023/1007/schedule/1)[[91]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng)

---

## Trend 8: Advanced sensing (presence/occupancy radar/vision) + battery optimization enables “ambient automation”
**Why**
- Matter 1.4 expands occupancy sensing methods and adds better tuning/history reporting, and improves ICD battery behavior [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/).  
- Energy harvesting sensors reduce maintenance and enable dense sensing [[75]](https://www.enocean.com/en/smart-building/motion-and-activity-sensor/).

**Product types**
- Presence/occupancy sensors (radar/vision/ambient)  
- Batteryless or ultra-low-power sensors/buttons  
- Multi-sensor fusion devices (presence + light + temperature/humidity)

**Features**
- Sensitivity tuning, event history, fewer false triggers [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)  
- Long-battery or batteryless operation [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)[[75]](https://www.enocean.com/en/smart-building/motion-and-activity-sensor/)  
- Privacy-aware design (avoid unnecessary biometrics; local inference where feasible) [[96]](https://www.edpb.europa.eu/sites/default/files/files/file1/edpb_guidelines_201903_video_devices_en_0.pdf)[[91]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng)

---

## Trend 9: Robots become better ecosystem citizens (Matter control) while autonomy competes on perception + mechanics
**Why**
- Matter includes robot vacuum support (earlier Matter revisions) and launches show vendors enabling Matter support across vacuums/mops [[19]](https://matter-smarthome.de/en/products/an-overview-of-matter-innovations-at-ifa-2025/).  
- AI navigation and structured-light/3D sensing continue to improve capability [[19]](https://matter-smarthome.de/en/products/an-overview-of-matter-innovations-at-ifa-2025/).

**Product types**
- Matter-enabled robot vacuums and mopping systems  
- Docking/maintenance automation accessories

**Features**
- Standardized start/stop/status + integration into routines/scenes [[19]](https://matter-smarthome.de/en/products/an-overview-of-matter-innovations-at-ifa-2025/)  
- AI navigation and obstacle handling [[19]](https://matter-smarthome.de/en/products/an-overview-of-matter-innovations-at-ifa-2025/)

---

## Trend 10: Lighting remains high volume, but splits into Wi‑Fi Matter at low cost and Thread Matter where reliability/battery matters; bridges remain relevant for advanced features
**Why**
- Lighting was among the earliest Matter categories, driving device availability and user familiarity [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)[[170]](https://www.the-ambient.com/best/best-matter-smart-home-devices-controllers-2977/).  
- Matter bridges still matter because vendor ecosystems can expose richer lighting effects than baseline Matter in many setups [[30]](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/matter/).  
- Retail indicates strong demand for lighting bridges/controllers [[153]](https://www.amazon.ca/Best-Sellers-Home-Automation/zgbs/hi/9716053011)[[168]](https://www.amazon.ca/Best-Sellers-Home-Automation-Hubs-Controllers/zgbs/hi/9716054011).

**Product types**
- Matter-over-Wi‑Fi smart bulbs/light strips for cost-down SKUs  
- Matter-over-Thread bulbs/switches where mesh reliability is critical  
- Lighting bridges that expose advanced features while providing Matter bridging

**Features**
- Command batching and scenes for synchronized experiences [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)  
- Continued vendor differentiation in dynamic scenes/entertainment modes (often via bridges) [[30]](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/matter/)

---

# Conclusion: the specific product types/features most likely to be “major trends” shaping smart home’s future (2026–2030)

If you need an actionable shortlist for product planning, the clearest major trends (with the concrete product directions they imply) are:

1) **Matter-based HEMS + energy telemetry everywhere**  
   - Products: HEMS hubs/apps, energy monitors, smart panels/load controllers, energy-aware appliances  
   - Features: real-time energy reporting, forecast-aware scheduling, demand-response hooks, EVSE integration [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)[[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)[[129]](https://www.clearesult.com/insights/expanding-demand-response-and-managed-charging-for-year-round-grid-stability)

2) **Electrification control stack (EV charging, heat pumps, water heaters) integrated into smart home platforms**  
   - Products: Matter EV chargers, thermostat-as-controller hubs, heat pump/water heater controllers  
   - Features: departure-time EV charging, preset/scheduled HVAC modes, device energy management modes [[32]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-3-specification-released/)[[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)

3) **AI-native security: cameras/doorbells that summarize, explain, and search—sold as subscriptions**  
   - Products: AI cameras/doorbells + security bundles  
   - Features: semantic alerts, natural-language video search, daily summaries, verification workflows [[20]](https://techcrunch.com/2024/10/09/amazon-revamps-ring-subscriptions-with-ai-video-search/)[[64]](https://techcrunch.com/2025/10/01/google-unveils-ai-powered-nest-indoor-and-outdoor-cameras-and-a-new-doorbell/)[[136]](https://support.google.com/googlenest/answer/16581343)

4) **Infrastructure renaissance: HRAP routers + Thread 1.4 border-router convergence + multi-admin experiences**  
   - Products: Matter-certified routers/APs with Thread border router, smart hubs/displays, multi-protocol bridges  
   - Features: credential sharing, unified Thread networks, higher commissioning success, local control and diagnostics [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)[[51]](https://www.threadgroup.org/Portals/0/Documents/Thread_1.4_Features_White_Paper_September_2024.pdf)[[36]](https://developer.apple.com/apple-home/downloads/Matter-Accessory-Best-Practices-for-Apple-Home.pdf)

5) **“Ambient access”: UWB/NFC/Matter locks aligned with cross-platform credential standards (Aliro direction)**  
   - Products: UWB-enabled smart locks and access readers  
   - Features: hands-free unlock with trajectory logic, stronger anti-relay posture, wallet-key interoperability [[67]](https://www.aqara.com/us/news-us-2/aqara-introduces-uwb-powered-smart-lock-u400-at-ces-2026/)[[169]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/the-connectivity-standards-alliance-announces-aliro-a-new-effort-to-make-mobile-devices-wearables-central-to-a-digital-access-future/)

6) **Dense sensing becomes practical: radar/vision occupancy + batteryless/ultra-low-power sensors enabling automation that “just happens”**  
   - Products: occupancy/presence sensors, energy harvesting sensors, ICD-optimized sensors/buttons  
   - Features: tunable presence sensing, event history, long idle time battery behaviors [[33]](https://csa-iot.org/newsroom/matter-1-4-enables-more-capable-smart-homes/)[[75]](https://www.enocean.com/en/smart-building/motion-and-activity-sensor/)

Across all of the above, a cross-cutting “meta-trend” will shape winners and losers: **compliance-grade security and lifecycle support** (unique credentials, published support periods, vulnerability reporting, secure updates, encryption, data minimization), driven by IoT labeling/regulation in the US/EU/UK/Singapore [[87]](https://docs.fcc.gov/public/attachments/FCC-24-26A1.pdf)[[90]](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2023/1007/schedule/1)[[91]](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/2847/oj/eng)[[92]](https://cdn.cta.tech/cta/media/media/pdfs/cta-cyber-trust-mark-webinar-part-i_june-2024_fcc_and_cta.pdf)[[94]](https://www.csa.gov.sg/our-programmes/certification-and-labelling-schemes/cybersecurity-labelling-scheme/for-consumers/).
